business_definition: Measures the percentage increase or decrease in net sales revenue over a specified period compared to the prior period.
kpi_description: Tracks overall top-line expansion, market penetration, and sales effectiveness.
formula: ((Current Period Net Sales - Prior Period Net Sales) / Prior Period Net Sales) * 100
numerator: Current Period Net Sales - Prior Period Net Sales
notes: Ensure currency conversion rates are standardized across global subsidiaries.

business_definition: Measures actual sales revenue generated compared to the budgeted sales target or quota for a given period.
kpi_description: Assesses target attainment, forecasting accuracy, and sales team performance.
formula: (Actual Sales Revenue / Budgeted Sales Target) * 100
notes: Target values are reset annually during the corporate budgeting cycle.

business_definition: Calculates actual cash generated from sales after deducting returns, trade discounts, rebates, and promotions.
kpi_description: Monitors gross-to-net leakage and the profitability of sales contracts.
formula: Gross Sales Revenue - (Returns + Discounts + Rebates + Promos)
numerator: Gross Sales Revenue - (Returns + Discounts + Rebates + Promos)
notes: Critical for detecting margin leakage from unapproved sales discounting.

business_definition: Calculates the average price at which a product or service is sold across transaction lines.
kpi_description: Tracks price erosion, product mix shifts, and pricing power in the market.
formula: Total Net Sales Revenue / Total Units Sold
recommended_threshold_range: Red < -2% YoY, Amber -2% to 0% YoY, Green >= 0% YoY
notes: Must be analyzed alongside sales volume to check price-elasticity of demand.

business_definition: Measures the velocity of new customer wins over a specified period.
kpi_description: Monitors business development success and marketing campaign effectiveness.
formula: (New Customers Won in Period / Active Customer Base at Start) * 100
notes: Define 'Active Customer' strictly to exclude single-transaction accounts.

business_definition: Measures the total volume of acceptable finished products produced by a manufacturing line per unit of time.
kpi_description: Evaluates line capacity, processing speed, and production velocity.
formula: Total Units Produced / Total Production Hours
notes: Ensure that rejected parts are deducted from the numerator.

business_definition: Measures the extent to which a plant or facility uses its productive capacity, comparing actual output to potential maximum output.
kpi_description: Highlights production efficiency, overhead absorption, and capacity constraints.
formula: (Actual Output / Rated Design Capacity) * 100
notes: Targeting 100% capacity utilization is discouraged as it removes scheduling buffers.

business_definition: Integrates Availability, Performance, and Quality rates to measure the overall efficiency of a machine or production line.
kpi_description: Standard manufacturing metric for assessing asset optimization and waste reduction.
formula: Availability Rate * Performance Rate * Quality Rate
numerator: Operating Time * Actual Throughput * Good Units
denominator: Planned Production Time * Ideal Run Rate * Total Units
notes: OEE is highly sensitive to correct logging of planned vs unplanned downtimes.

business_definition: Measures the percentage of products that are manufactured correctly to specifications on the first attempt without rework, scrap, or inspection failure.
kpi_description: Indicates production quality stability and eliminates cost of poor quality.
formula: (Good Units Produced with No Rework / Total Units Started) * 100
numerator: Good Units Produced with No Rework
notes: Reworked products that are eventually sold still count as failures for FPY.

business_definition: Calculates the total cost incurred (materials, labor, and factory overhead) to produce a single finished unit.
kpi_description: Assesses cost management, direct efficiency, and scale absorption.
formula: Total Manufacturing Cost in Period / Total Good Units Produced
numerator: Direct Materials + Direct Labor + Applied Overhead
recommended_threshold_range: Red > +2% YoY, Amber 0% to 2% YoY, Green <= 0% YoY
notes: Excludes general corporate overhead, marketing, and interest expenses.

business_definition: Measures the average number of days that capital is tied up in raw materials, work-in-progress, and finished goods inventory before sales.
kpi_description: Indicates inventory management efficiency and working capital velocity.
formula: (Average Inventory Value / Annual Cost of Goods Sold) * 365
recommended_threshold_range: Red > 55 Days, Amber 45-55 Days, Green <= 45 Days
notes: Low inventory days improve cash flow but increase stockout risks.

business_definition: Measures the percentage of customer orders delivered completely (In-Full) and within the scheduled window (On-Time) at the client premises.
kpi_description: Evaluates outbound logistics capability and customer delivery satisfaction.
formula: (Orders Delivered On-Time and In-Full / Total Orders Shipped) * 100
notes: Must align definitions of 'On-Time' (e.g. 30-min window) with key retailers.

business_definition: Measures the percentage of purchase order lines delivered by raw material suppliers on or before the committed delivery date.
kpi_description: Evaluates upstream supply reliability and supplier performance.
formula: (PO Lines Delivered On-Time / Total PO Lines Delivered) * 100
notes: Critical input for setting safety stock levels in material requirements planning.

business_definition: Measures the accuracy of the demand forecast compared to actual sales units, calculated using mean absolute percentage error.
kpi_description: Highlights supply chain planning efficiency and minimizes stockout/inventory write-off risk.
formula: 100 - Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE)
numerator: 100 - (Sum of Absolute Errors / Total Actual Units)
notes: Calculated at the Product Family / Region level to avoid low-volume skewing.

business_definition: Measures total outbound logistics costs (warehousing, freight, and distribution) as a percentage of total net sales revenue.
kpi_description: Indicates supply chain financial efficiency and shipping cost controls.
formula: (Total Logistics and Freight Cost / Total Net Sales Revenue) * 100
notes: Highly volatile to global fuel surcharges and carrier spot market rates.

business_definition: Measures operating profitability as a percentage of revenue, excluding the impact of financing, tax, and non-cash depreciation/amortization expenses.
kpi_description: Assesses pure operating margin, cost controls, and EBITDA performance.
formula: (EBITDA / Total Net Revenue) * 100
numerator: Earnings before Interest, Tax, Depreciation, and Amortization
notes: The primary operational profitability metric for corporate valuations and covenants.

business_definition: Measures the number of days it takes for a company to convert its investments in inventory and other resources into cash flows from sales.
kpi_description: The ultimate benchmark of working capital management efficiency.
formula: Days Inventory Outstanding (DIO) + Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) - Days Payable Outstanding (DPO)
recommended_threshold_range: Red > 65 Days, Amber 50-65 Days, Green <= 50 Days
notes: A negative cash conversion cycle is possible in retail contexts.

business_definition: Measures the average number of days it takes for a company to collect cash from customers after credit sales are invoiced.
kpi_description: Tracks credit collection performance, accounts receivable efficiency, and bad debt risks.
formula: (Average Accounts Receivable / Total Credit Sales) * 365
recommended_threshold_range: Red > 50 Days, Amber 42-50 Days, Green <= 42 Days
notes: Should be tracked by customer segment and region to isolate delayed billing.

business_definition: Calculates short-term liquidity, evaluating current assets against current liabilities.
kpi_description: Assesses solvency, operational liquidity, and ability to meet short-term liabilities.
formula: Total Current Assets / Total Current Liabilities
denominator: Accounts Payable + Accrued Expenses + Short Term Debt
notes: Ratios > 2.0 might indicate excess idle inventory or unutilized cash balances.

business_definition: Measures the percentage of produced items that fail to meet internal quality inspection parameters or drawing specifications.
kpi_description: Indicates quality control capability and raw manufacturing scrap metrics.
formula: (Total Defective Units / Total Units Checked) * 100
notes: Can be computed as PPM (Parts Per Million) for high-precision components.

business_definition: Calculates the total financial loss resulting from quality failures, including internal scrap/rework and external warranty/claims.
kpi_description: Highlights margin dilution caused by manufacturing process variance and quality failure.
formula: Scrap Costs + Rework Costs + Warranty Claims + Customer Returns Cost
numerator: Material Scrap + Direct Rework Labor + Invoiced Warranty Claims
notes: Typically categorized into prevention, appraisal, internal failure, and external failure costs.

business_definition: Measures the number of customer quality complaints submitted relative to the total volume of orders shipped.
kpi_description: Direct indicator of product quality gaps and post-delivery client satisfaction.
formula: (Customer Complaints Received / Total Orders Shipped) * 100
notes: Every complaint must trigger an automated root-cause analysis (8D report).

business_definition: Measures customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend products/services based on standard 0-10 user scoring surveys.
kpi_description: Indicates brand health, customer loyalty, and retention velocity.
formula: % Promoters (score 9-10) - % Detractors (score 0-6)
notes: Must be backed by transactional surveys shortly after service events.

business_definition: Measures the percentage of total corporate procurement transactions conducted outside approved supplier contracts or agreements.
kpi_description: Highlights purchasing compliance risks and contract leakage.
formula: (Procurement Value Outside Contract / Total Procurement Spend) * 100
notes: Maverick spend directly correlates with lost discount and volume rebate opportunities.

business_definition: Measures the average elapsed hours from purchase requisition submission to purchase order dispatch to the vendor.
kpi_description: Monitors purchasing operations speed and approval bottlenecks.
formula: Sum of (PO Dispatch Time - PR Submission Time) / Total POs Dispatched
numerator: Sum of Hours from PR Submission to PO Dispatch
recommended_threshold_range: Red > 72 Hours, Amber 24-72 Hours, Green <= 24 Hours
notes: Catalogs can reduce this cycle time to under 1 hour via auto-approvals.

business_definition: Measures greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1 and Scope 2) normalized by business activity (metric tons of CO2 equivalent per unit of production or revenue).
kpi_description: Tracks sustainability compliance, regulatory exposure, and green manufacturing progress.
formula: Metric Tons of CO2 equivalent (Scope 1 & 2) / Total Production Units
recommended_threshold_range: Red >= 0% YoY, Amber -5% to 0% YoY, Green <= -5% YoY
notes: Scope 3 emissions (supply chain value loop) can be appended as compliance reporting matures.

business_definition: Calculates the number of recordable occupational injuries and illnesses per 200,000 work hours (standard for 100 full-time workers per year).
kpi_description: Indicates workforce safety compliance, operational risks, and safety culture health.
formula: (Number of Recordable Safety Incidents * 200,000) / Total Employee Hours Worked
denominator: Total Employee Hours Worked (Employees and Contractors)
notes: Zero incidents is the absolute operational goal across all facilities.
